epics.title: Authentication System
epics.description: Implement user authentication with OAuth2, session management, and role-based access control.
successCriteria: Users can sign in via Google and GitHub OAuth
Admin/user roles enforced on all endpoints

research.content:
## OAuth2 Flow

Using Authorization Code flow with PKCE for security.
- Google: `accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth`
- GitHub: `github.com/login/oauth/authorize`

### Token Storage
JWT stored in httpOnly cookies, refresh tokens in database with rotation.

epics.title: API Rate Limiting & Caching
epics.description: Add Redis-backed rate limiting and response caching to protect API endpoints from abuse and improve performance.
successCriteria: Rate limits enforced per-user and per-IP
Cache hit ratio above 80% for read endpoints
P99 latency under 50ms for cached responses

issues.title: Live-updating web dashboard with `/project-serve` command
issues.description:
Add a `/project-serve` command that starts a local web server serving a read-only, live-updating dashboard of the project's epics, issues, and assets. Uses [json-render](https://github.com/vercel-labs/json-render) with shadcn components for rendering the project database. Every element has a "copy reference" button so users can paste precise references back into the agent chat.

**Architecture:**
- **Server (extension-side):** Node.js `http` module — serves static HTML + SSE endpoint. Watches `database.json` for changes and pushes updates.
- **Client (browser):** Single HTML page loading React + `@json-render/shadcn` from CDN (esm.sh). Renders a spec generated from the database. SSE connection for live updates.
- **No npm dependencies** — everything client-side loads via CDN, server uses only Node.js built-ins.

**Multi-project support:**
- Auto-finds an available port (starting from 3100, incrementing)
- URL includes a project identifier derived from cwd, e.g. `http://localhost:3142/project/a1b2c3/`

**Copy-reference:**
- Each element (epic, issue, asset, todo, research note) has a hover "📋" button
- Copies a reference like `[epic:abc123] Title` for pasting into agent chat

**Files affected:**
- New `dashboard-server.ts` — HTTP server, file watcher, SSE, spec generation
- `commands.ts` — register `/project-serve` command
- `index.ts` — wire up the new command

research.content:
## json-render Research

### What it is
json-render is a "Generative UI" framework from Vercel Labs. You define a **catalog** of components with typed props (via Zod), then render a JSON **spec** (tree of `{type, props, children}` elements) through a **registry** of component implementations.

### Key package: `@json-render/shadcn`
- 36 pre-built shadcn/ui components: Card, Stack, Grid, Heading, Text, Badge, Table, Accordion, Alert, Progress, Separator, Button, Input, Select, etc.
- Perfect for a read-only dashboard — Table, Card, Badge, Stack, Heading, Text, Alert, Accordion, Progress cover all our needs.

### Architecture Challenge
json-render is a **React framework** — it needs React, ReactDOM, and a build step (or CDN). The pi extension runs as interpreted TypeScript inside the agent. We need to bridge these:

**Proposed approach:**
1. **Server side (extension):** Use Node.js `http` module to start a server. Serve static HTML + SSE endpoint for live updates. Watch `database.json` for changes.
2. **Client side (browser):** A single HTML file that loads React + json-render + shadcn from CDN (esm.sh or unpkg). Renders the project database as a json-render spec. Connects to SSE for live updates.

### Spec Generation
The extension will need a `specFromDatabase()` function that transforms `database.json` into a json-render spec:
- Epics → Card with status badges, todos as checklists, linked issues
- Issues → Table rows or Cards with type icons, status badges
- Assets → Grouped by category in Accordion sections
- Each element gets a `data-ref` attribute for copy-reference functionality

### Multi-project / Unique URLs
- Auto-find available port (try 3100+, increment)
- URL path includes project identifier (hash of cwd or project name)
- e.g. `http://localhost:3142/project/a1b2c3/`

### Dependencies concern
- No npm dependencies needed in the extension itself — everything loads via CDN on the client
- Server is just Node.js `http` + `fs.watch`
- The spec JSON is generated server-side from the database

## Available shadcn Components (relevant subset)

| Component | Use case in dashboard |
|-----------|----------------------|
| **Card** | Epic cards, issue cards, asset cards |
| **Stack** | Layout — vertical/horizontal grouping |
| **Grid** | Multi-column layouts (e.g. epic grid) |
| **Heading** | Section headers (h1-h3) |
| **Text** | Descriptions, body text, muted captions |
| **Badge** | Status labels (draft/ready/in-progress/closed), issue types (bug/feature/chore) |
| **Table** | Issue lists, asset lists |
| **Accordion** | Collapsible sections (research notes, asset categories) |
| **Alert** | Warnings, info banners |
| **Progress** | Epic completion (todos done/total) |
| **Separator** | Visual dividers |
| **Tabs** | Switch between Epics / Issues / Assets views |

### Copy-Reference Design
Each rendered element wraps in a container with:
- A small "📋" button on hover
- Clicking copies a reference like `[epic:abc123] Title` or `[issue:def456] Title`
- User pastes this into agent chat for precise linking

autoValidation.strategy: User confirms the dashboard serves, live-updates, and copy-reference works in browser

issues.title: Google OAuth2 integration
issues.description: Implement Google OAuth2 sign-in flow with PKCE. Handle token exchange, profile fetching, and account creation for new users.

issues.title: GitHub OAuth2 integration
issues.description: Implement GitHub OAuth2 sign-in with org-based access restrictions. Support linking GitHub account to existing user.

questions.text: Should we support GitHub Enterprise or only github.com?
questions.answer: Start with github.com only. GitHub Enterprise support can be added later as a separate feature.

questions.text: Do we need org-level access restrictions from day one?

issues.title: Session tokens not invalidated on password change
issues.description:
When a user changes their password, existing JWT tokens remain valid. All active sessions should be invalidated and the user forced to re-authenticate.

**Steps to reproduce:**
1. Sign in on two devices
2. Change password on device A
3. Device B still has valid session

**Expected:** Device B session invalidated.
issues.closeMessage: Fixed by adding token version to JWT claims. On password change, user's token version is incremented, invalidating all existing tokens.

validations.criterion:
When a user changes their password, existing JWT tokens remain valid. All active sessions should be invalidated and the user forced to re-authenticate.

**Steps to reproduce:**
1. Sign in on two devices
2. Change password on device A
3. Device B still has valid session

**Expected:** Device B session invalidated.
validations.evidence: Added token version check in auth middleware. Tests verify old tokens are rejected after password change.

issues.title: Add auth middleware test coverage
issues.description:
Current test coverage for `src/middleware/auth.ts` is at 34%. Need to add unit tests for:
- Token validation edge cases
- Role checking
- Expired token handling
- Missing auth header scenarios

issues.title: Evaluate passkey/WebAuthn support
issues.description: Research feasibility of adding WebAuthn/passkey support as a passwordless authentication option. Evaluate libraries, browser support, and UX implications.

research.content:
## WebAuthn Browser Support

| Browser | Support |
|---------|--------|
| Chrome 67+ | ✅ Full |
| Firefox 60+ | ✅ Full |
| Safari 14+ | ✅ Full |
| Edge 18+ | ✅ Full |

### Libraries
- **@simplewebauthn/server** — most popular, 2.3k stars
- **fido2-lib** — lower level, more control

### Considerations
- Need to handle platform authenticator vs roaming authenticator
- Recovery flow when device is lost is complex
Talked with the team — consensus is to defer passkey support to v2. Focus on OAuth first, then layer on WebAuthn as an optional MFA method.

issues.title: Dark mode toggle in dashboard header
issues.description: Add a light/dark mode toggle to the web dashboard. Currently hardcoded to dark theme. Some users may prefer light mode for readability or printing.

issues.title: Dashboard SSE reconnect drops first update
issues.description:
After SSE reconnects, the first `database.json` change is sometimes missed because the file watcher event fires before the new SSE connection is established.

**Impact:** Users see stale data for one update cycle after reconnect.

assets.title: Core Tech Stack
assets.context: Reference when making technology or architecture decisions
assets.body:
## Core Stack

- **Runtime:** Node.js 22 (LTS)
- **Language:** TypeScript 5.7+ (strict mode)
- **Framework:** None (vanilla HTTP server)
- **Database:** SQLite via better-sqlite3
- **Cache:** Redis 7+
- **Auth:** JWT + OAuth2 (Google, GitHub)

## Conventions
- ESM modules only (`type: \"module\"`)
- No classes — prefer functions and closures
- Zod for runtime validation
- Vitest for testing

assets.title: Code Review Checklist
assets.context: Apply when reviewing or submitting code changes
assets.body:
## Review Checklist

- [ ] Types are explicit, no `any`
- [ ] Error handling covers edge cases
- [ ] New functions have JSDoc comments
- [ ] Tests cover happy path and error cases
- [ ] No console.log left in production code
- [ ] Breaking changes documented in CHANGELOG

assets.title: Project Goals Q1 2026
assets.context: Reference when prioritizing features or making scope decisions
assets.body:
## Q1 2026 Goals

1. **Ship auth system** — OAuth2 login for Google and GitHub
2. **API stability** — Rate limiting and caching in place
3. **Developer experience** — Live dashboard for project visibility
4. **Quality bar** — 80%+ test coverage on critical paths
